params.name: Ra-Hotep Couple
params.description: This statue was created during the Fourth Dynasty of ancient Egypt and is a classic example of a double seated statue. In the statue, Prince Rahotep and Princess Nofret sit side by side, with a dignified and elegant demeanor, strictly adhering to the frontal symmetry principle of Egyptian art. This principle dictates that figures are presented facing forward, with limbs close to the body, conveying the dignity and solemnity of the royal family members. Although this statue is not of a pharaoh, a …
